vulnerability.title: Add noexec Option to /home
vulnerability.description: The noexec mount option can be used to prevent binaries from being executed out of /home. Add the noexec option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /home.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then function perform_remediation { # the mount point /home has to be defined in /etc/fstab # before this remediation can be executed. In case it is not defined, the # remediation aborts and no changes regarding the mount point are done. mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" "/home")" grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" -q /etc/fstab \ || { echo "The mount point '/home' is not even in /etc/fstab, so we can't set up mount options" >&2; echo "Not

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only edits /etc/fstab to add the noexec option for /home, which does not affect system authentication, network services, or bootloader configuration. While it may prevent execution of user‑stored binaries, this is an intended security measure and does not risk locking out SSH or console access.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; User scripts or binaries in /home may fail to execute; Potential need to remount or reboot for the change to take effect

# /etc/fstab
# Created by anaconda on Mon Feb  9 07:40:38 2026
#
# Accessible filesystems, by reference, are maintained under '/dev/disk/'.

#

# units generated from this file.

/dev/mapper/rlm-root    /                       xfs     defaults        0 0
UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs     defaults,nodev        0 0
UUID=F56C-228D          /boot/efi               vfat    umask=0077,shortname=winnt,nosuid 0 2
/dev/mapper/rlm-home    /home                   xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/rlm-swap    none                    swap    defaults        0 0
tmpfs /dev/shm tmpfs defaults,nodev,nosuid 0 0

llm_verdict.message:
The **/home** entry in **/etc/fstab** now includes the `noexec` option:

```
/dev/mapper/rlm-home    /home    xfs    defaults,noexec    0 0
```

This satisfies the OpenSCAP rule **mount_option_home_noexec**, ensuring that execution of binaries from `/home` is prohibited. No additional modifications were made.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation correctly adds the `noexec` option to the /home entry in /etc/fstab, backs up the original file, validates the syntax with `mount -fav` and is idempotent. It therefore satisfies the OpenSCAP rule. The approach is functional but could be streamlined and hardened.
concerns: The sed regular expressions are complex and may fail on unusual whitespace or commented lines, potentially corrupting the fstab entry.
The script does not attempt to remount /home with the new option immediately; the change only takes effect after a reboot.
The initial platform detection logic is extensive and may incorrectly skip remediation on some supported platforms.
Using `mount -fav` re‑mounts all filesystems, which could cause temporary disruptions on systems with sensitive mounts.
suggested_improvements: Replace the sed regex with a more robust parsing method (e.g., using awk or a dedicated fstab manipulation tool) to handle edge‑case formatting safely.
After editing fstab, perform a targeted `mount -o remount,noexec /home` to apply the new option without requiring a reboot.
Simplify or document the platform‑check block to avoid accidental exclusion of valid environments.
Add explicit error handling to restore the backup automatically if `mount -fav` reports an error.
